cannotConnectToServerDeletionRequestQueu.message: Cannot connect to the OneTab server. Please check your internet connection. Your shared page deletion request has been queued locally and will be automatically transmitted to the OneTab server as soon as it is reachable again.

cannotMixTabsGroupsFoldersDuringDrag.message: It's not possible to mix tabs, groups and folders when dragging. This is because, for example, you can't drag a folder into the same places you can drag a tab.

cannotMoveItemAncestorHere.message: A parent item can’t be moved into its own child, because that would create a loop.

cannotMoveItemIntoItself.message: An item can’t be moved into itself.

canPasteWebContentForExample.message: You can paste text copied from a web page or document, and links will be automatically extracted. For example:

completedTaskFlagHelp.message: This item is a task. Click this icon to mark it as pending (not completed).

completeEditingShortcutInfo.message: When editing a notes field, use this shortcut to complete editing.

conflictingShortcutsHint.message: If any of the keyboard shortcuts above do not work or conflict with any other applications, you can configure a different shortcut key.

copyToClipboardMenuItemHelp.message: Copy these web links to the clipboard, so you can paste them into other applications. You can change the clipboard text format in the OneTab Options page.

createFolderWithinMenuItemHelp.message: Folders are for organizing groups of tabs. Folders contain groups, and groups contain tabs.

createGroupInsideDestinationHelp.message: If unticked, your tabs will be added directly into the destination group. If ticked, a new browser tab group will be created for your tabs within the destination group. Only tick this box if you want to use your browser's "tab groups" feature.

createGroupMenuItemHelp.message: Create a new, empty group. A group contains a list of tabs, which can collectively be restored into a window.

createNewGroupTip.message: To create a new group, first untick "Show folders only". This is so you will be able to see the newly created group in the list.

dontCloseGroupedTabs.message: Don't close any tabs within tab groups

dontCloseTabsFromDomains.message: Don't close any tabs from EXAMPLE.COM

dragCopyModifierInfo.message: Hold the ctrl or alt key when dropping a tab to create a copy of it instead of moving it.

dragTabsHereForQuickList.message: Drag your stored tabs or groups here. They will be added to your quick list.

editTabGroupTitleAndNotes.message: Edit the tab group title and notes

emojiShortcutInfo.message: When editing a notes field, use this shortcut to open the emoji picker.

emptyOneTabInfoMsg.message: When you have multiple tabs open, click the OneTab icon on your browser toolbar and your open tabs will appear here.

encryptedSyncAndBackupComingSoonMessage.message:
Coming soon: You will be able to sync your OneTab data across multiple browser profiles and computers.
OneTab has been completely rewritten so that changes made on one device will instantly appear on all other devices.
If you use this feature, your data will be end-to-end encrypted with high-strength AES-256 encryption. This makes it impossible for the OneTab developers to see the tabs you have backed up or synced between devices, because only you have the encryption key.

exportThenImportNote.message: The following list of URLs can also be imported back into OneTab on this or a different computer

firefoxShortcutConfigInstructions.message: Enter the URL "about:addons?type=extension" into your browser address bar. When the page appears, click the cog icon, then choose "Manage Extension Shortcuts"

importLinksHereMenuItemHelp.message: You can paste in a list of web links, or import browser bookmarks.

incogitoPermissionRequired.message: OneTab needs permission to create incognito windows.

lockedGroupUnlockHelp.message: To unlock, use the More… menu. When a group is locked, you cannot delete the group or its tabs, and tabs cannot be reordered.

lockMenuItemHelp.message: When a group is locked, you cannot remove or reorder items within the group (until you unlock the group).

manifestDescription.message: Save up to 95% memory and reduce tab clutter

markAsArchivedMenuItemHelp.message: When you mark an item as archived, it will have a gray background. This is a visual indicator that you have finished with this item (for example, it is an article you have finished reading). If you archive something that is marked as a task, the task will no longer appear in the tasks column in your OneTab page.

markAsDoneTaskMenuItemHelp.message: Visually indicate that a task has been completed

markAsPendingTaskMenuItemHelp.message: Visually indicate that this item is a task. A task icon will appear, which you can then click to visually indicate that the task has been completed. All tasks (unless marked as archived) will also appear in the tasks column in your OneTab page.

optionAutoActionOnOpenArchiveDesc.message: Marking tabs as archived gives them a gray striped background. This provides a visual indication that you have read or finished with the tabs.

optionAutoActionOnOpenNote.message: Locked groups always keep their tabs

optionAutoActionOnOpenOpenDesc.message: To mark the tabs as archived when restoring them, hold the Alt or Option key

optionAutoActionOnOpenTrashDesc.message: If you hold the Ctrl or Cmd key, the tab(s) will remain in your list.

optionBrowserActionHeading.message: When you click the OneTab toolbar icon:

optionBrowserActionOpenPopupDesc.message: The action popup will show you a list of open tabs, and let you choose exactly which tabs you want to send to OneTab. It will let you set a title for the group of tabs you're storing, so you don't have to do this later within the OneTab page. The popup will also give you easy access to your quick list from any browser window.

optionBrowserActionOpenPopupDesc2.message: Choose exactly which tabs to store

optionBrowserActionSendCurrentTabDesc.message: The tab will be placed inside the first group on your OneTab page (ignoring any groups which are pinned or locked)

optionBrowserActionSendCurrentTabTitle.message: Send only the current tab to OneTab

optionBrowserActionSendTabsInWindowDesc.message: Your tabs will be closed and stored in your OneTab list. If you have selected multiple tabs in your browser's tab bar (using the Cmd, Ctrl or Shift key), only the selected tabs will be sent to OneTab. If you are using your browser's tab groups feature, only the current tab group will be sent to OneTab.

optionBrowserActionSendTabsInWindowDesc2.message: The quickest way to store tabs

optionBrowserActionSendTabsInWindowTitle.message: Send tabs in the current window to OneTab

optionCopyToClipboardFormatNote.message: This setting applies when you use the 'Copy to clipboard' action. You can also drag tabs and groups directly from OneTab into a document.

optionCopyToClipboardFormatRichTextDesc.message: Applications such as word processors will allow you to paste rich text. If not supported, plain text will be pasted instead.

optionDefaultTabGroupOpenTypeCurrentWind.message: Open the browser tab group in the current window

optionDefaultTabGroupOpenTypeHeading.message: When clicking to open a group of tabs that are configured to open as a "browser tab group":

optionDefaultTabGroupOpenTypeNewWindow.message: Open the browser tab group in a new window

optionDefaultTabGroupOpenTypeNote.message: If you use the dropdown to choose a particular option (such as to open tabs in an incognito window), that option will be remembered for that particular group.

optionDefaultWindowGroupOpenTypeCurrentW.message: Open the tabs in the current window

optionDefaultWindowGroupOpenTypeHeading.message: When restoring a group of tabs:

optionDefaultWindowGroupOpenTypeNewWindo.message: Open the tabs in a new window

optionDefaultWindowGroupOpenTypeNote.message: If you use the dropdown to choose a particular option (such as to open tabs in an incognito window), that option will be remembered for that particular group.

optionDiscardNonActiveTabOnOpenHeading.message: Save memory (RAM) immediately when tabs are first opened:

optionDiscardNonActiveTabOnOpenNote.message: If this setting is enabled, it will immediately unload tabs from memory when you first open them from your OneTab list. This is useful if you frequently open dozens of tabs at once, as it will prevent them all from being loaded into memory until you access them. If this setting is disabled, many web browsers will still automatically choose to unload tabs from memory if you are not currently using them.

optionDisplayContextMenuHeading.message: Right-click inside a web page to access the OneTab context menu:

optionDisplayContextMenuNote.message: If you disable the menu, you will not be able to access the "Exclude website from OneTab" feature

optionDuplicatesRejectDesc.message: If OneTab already contains the tab, it will not be added again.

optionMoveOneTabToCurrentWindowOnOpenFal.message: Switch to the window where the OneTab tab is already open

optionMoveOneTabToCurrentWindowOnOpenTru.message: Move any existing OneTab tab to the current window

optionPinnedTabsAllow.message: Allow pinned tabs to be sent to OneTab

optionPinnedTabsDontSend.message: Don't send pinned tabs to OneTab

optionPinnedTabsDontSendDesc.message: You can still manually send a pinned tab to OneTab by right clicking within the web page to access the OneTab menu, and then clicking 'Send only this tab to OneTab'

optionPinnedTabsNote.message: Note:  A tab becomes 'pinned' when you right click on the tab and click 'Pin tab'. Some people like to make sites such as Facebook or Gmail pinned so they can easily locate them. OneTab will remember whether a tab was pinned when you restore it.

optionStartupLaunchNoneDesc3.message: To open OneTab manually, you can right-click inside any web page, and click 'Open OneTab' inside the OneTab menu.

optionSwitchToOpenedTabFalseTitle.message: Open the tab and stay in OneTab

optionSwitchToOpenedTabHeading.message: When restoring tabs from the OneTab page:

optionSwitchToOpenedTabNote.message: If you hold shift when clicking, the tab will open in a new window

optionSwitchToOpenedTabTrueTitle.message: Open the tab, and switch to it (unless you hold Command ⌘/Ctrl when clicking)

pasteRichOrPlainTextBelow.message: You can paste rich text or plain text below.

pendingTaskFlagHelp.message: This item is a task. Click this icon to mark it as completed.

permissionRequiredPleaseTryAgain.message: To create a shared page that can be accessed from any computer, permission is required to coordinate with the OneTab web server. Please try again.

pinToTopOfFolderMenuItemHelp.message: Pin this group so it’s always listed at the top of the folder

placeholderExplanation.message: Due to web browser security rules, OneTab is not allowed to directly restore this page. Please copy your URL and paste it into your browser's address bar.

plainTextLineFormat.message: If you paste plain text, ensure that each URL has a protocol specified (https://wikipedia.org, instead of just wikipedia.org). Each line should be in the following format:

popupSlashShortcutInfo.message: When inside the OneTab action popup (which appears when you click the OneTab icon), press the slash key to open the destination chooser and focus the search box.

printUseSaveTabsAsHtmlFileAlertHtml.message: For best printing results, use the Save as HTML file action in the folder's More… menu, then print the downloaded HTML file.

privacyAssuranceDetail.message: Your list of tabs is never transmitted or disclosed to either the OneTab developers or any other party. The only exception to this is if you intentionally use the 'share as a web page' feature to upload your list of tabs into a web page in order to share them with others.

privacyFirstMessage.message:
OneTab has been designed to protect your private data. Your tabs are only stored on your computer, and are never shared with the OneTab developers or any other party.
If you use our backup/sync feature, your data will be end-to-end encrypted with high-strength AES-256 encryption. This makes it impossible for the OneTab developers to see the tabs you have backed up or synced between devices, because only you have the encryption key. 
The only exception is if you intentionally decide to share some of your tabs with other people using our "Share as a web page" feature.
We believe in Privacy First.

readingListPermissionNecesssary.message: The reading list permission is necessary to allow OneTab to import items from your reading list. If this feature is not working, your browser may not support reading lists.
